title: Front-Chasing Refinement: Equidistribute a Fixed Cell Budget Against an Estimated Error Indicator
strategies: uniform grid: ignore the probe data, cut the domain into B equal-width cells (the reference)
top-k raw curvature: compute a second-difference indicator at every interior probe node and put a cut at the (B-1) largest values, with no budget accounting or recycling

equidistributing DP: treat the probe samples as a proxy field and find the exact partition of the base blocks into B groups that minimizes total within-group probe variance -- the closed-form fixed point of coarsen-the-flat / refine-the-front / recycle-the-freed-cell

iterative coarsen-refine loop: repeatedly estimate a per-cell error indicator from local probe samples, split the worst cell, and merge the two lowest-indicator adjacent cells to pay for it, holding the total cell count fixed
